description_long: This is a two-story timber-framed barn with a steeply pitched gable roof, reminiscent of medieval European architecture. The exterior features a prominent grid of dark wooden beams set against light infill panels, creating a striking half-timbered appearance. The barn has multiple small rectangular windows arranged in rows on both levels, providing natural light to the interior. The front facade includes an open bay or passageway on the lower right, likely serving as an entry or access point for equipment or livestock. The roof is constructed with overlapping planks, giving it a robust and weather-resistant look. The overall structure is tall and narrow, with a symmetrical design and strong vertical and diagonal timber elements.
description: A tall, narrow, half-timbered barn with a steep gable roof, multiple small windows, and a prominent timber grid exterior. The front features an open bay for entry or access.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with a symmetrical timber grid, several small windows, and an open bay on the lower right.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the solid gable roof and a row of small windows on both levels, with the timber framing continuing along the side.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front with a similar timber grid and window arrangement, but lacks the open bay entry.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing the gable roof and rows of windows, with consistent timber framing.
attribute_door_style: single large open bay entry, no visible doors
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small rectangular windows arranged in horizontal rows on both levels, evenly spaced
